sections.heading: This Directive establishes minimum rules concerning the definition of criminal offences and sanctions in the area of
sections.summary: This section requires Member States to implement national laws, regulations, and administrative provisions to comply with the GDPR by 3 December 2020. They must inform the Commission of these measures and include a reference to the Directive in their official publications.

requirements.text: Member States shall bring into force the laws, regulations and administrative provisions necessary to comply with this Directive by 3 December 2020.
Member States shall immediately inform the Commission thereof.
When Member States adopt those measures, they shall contain a reference to this Directive or be accompanied by such a reference on the occasion of their official publication.

sections.source_quote: Member States shall bring into force the laws, regulations and administrative provisions necessary to comply with this Directive by 3 December 2020.

sections.heading: This Directive does not apply to money laundering as regards property derived from criminal offences affecting the
sections.summary: This section mandates Member States to communicate the text of national laws adopted in the field covered by the GDPR to the Commission. The Commission must submit reports by 3 December 2022 and 3 December 2023 assessing compliance and impact on money laundering and fundamental rights.

requirements.text: Member States shall communicate to the Commission the text of the main provisions of national law which they adopt in the field covered by this Directive.

The Commission shall, by 3 December 2022, submit a report to the European Parliament and to the Council, assessing the extent to which the Member States have taken the necessary measures to comply with this Directive.

The Commission shall, by 3 December 2023, submit a report to the European Parliament and to the Council assessing the added value of this Directive with regard to combating money laundering as well as its impact on fundamental rights and freedoms.
The Commission shall take into account the information provided by Member States.

sections.source_quote: Member States shall communicate to the Commission the text of the main provisions of national law which they adopt in the field covered by this Directive.

sections.heading: Member States shall take the necessary measures to ensure that:
sections.summary: This section states that where an offence referred to in Articles 3 and 4 falls within the jurisdiction of more than one Member State and where any of the Member States concerned can validly prosecute on the basis of the same facts, the Member States concerned shall cooperate in order to decide which of them will prosecute the offender, with the aim of centralising proceedings in a single Member State. The matter shall, where appropriate and in accordance with Article 12 of Framework Decision 2009/948/JHA, be referred to Eurojust.

requirements.text: Where an offence referred to in Articles 3 and 4 falls within the jurisdiction of more than one Member State and where any of the Member States concerned can validly prosecute on the basis of the same facts, the Member States concerned shall cooperate in order to decide which of them will prosecute the offender, with the aim of centralising proceedings in a single Member State. The matter shall, where appropriate and in accordance with Article 12 of Framework Decision 2009/948/JHA, be referred to Eurojust.

sections.heading: In the case of point (c) of paragraph 3 of this Article, Member States may further require that the relevant conduct
sections.summary: This section allows Member States to require that the relevant conduct in point (c) of paragraph 3 constitutes a criminal offence under the national law of the other Member State or third country, except where the conduct constitutes one of the specified offences under Article 2.

requirements.text: In the case of point (c) of paragraph 3 of this Article, Member States may further require that the relevant conduct constitutes a criminal offence under the national law of the other Member State or of the third country where that conduct was committed, except where that conduct constitutes one of the offences referred to in points (a) to (e) and (h) of point (1) of Article 2 and as defined in the applicable Union law.

sections.source_quote: In the case of point (c) of paragraph 3 of this Article, Member States may further require that the relevant conduct constitutes a criminal offence under the national law of the other Member State or of the third country where that conduct was committed, except where that conduct constitutes one of the offences referred to in points (a) to (e) and (h) of point (1) of Article 2 and as defined in the applicable Union law.

sections.summary: This section requires Member States to take necessary measures to ensure that the conduct referred to in points (a) and (b) of paragraph 1 is punishable as a criminal offence when committed by persons who committed, or were involved in, the criminal activity from which the property was derived.
